reason: The text contains multiple regular human characters with names and/or professions and dialogue. Moses (מֹשֶׁה) is named and speaks multiple times with directives to the people. Pharaoh (פַּרְעֹה מֶלֶךְ מִצְרַיִם) is named with his title and has agency throughout. Pharaoh's servants ('ֲבָדָיו) are identified by profession and have dialogue ('מַה־ זֹּאת עָשִׂינוּ' - 'What have we done?'). The Israelites (בְנֵי־ יִשְׂרָאֵל/הָעָם) speak as a collective people with direct dialogue expressing their fears and concerns about the Egyptian pursuit. These are all regular human characters, not solely divine agents, and they possess identifiable names, titles, or collective identities along with direct speech.
